MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
Off Calendar May-16-2013 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES:
Lindsay Weiss of COOLEY MANION JONES LLP (213-622-7300), appearing for defendant / moving party Temporary Plant Cleaners, Inc.
Jennifer L. Alesio of BRAYTON & PURCELL (415-898-1555), appearing for plaintiffs / opposing parties William Thomas, et al.

RULING:
DEFENDANT TEMPORARY PLANT CLEANERS, INC.'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT

Hearing held and tentative ruling adopted: Defendant Temporary Plant Cleaners, Inc.'s motion for summary judgment is granted. Defendant sustained its initial burden. Plaintiffs failed to present admissible evidence creating a triable issue whether Mr. Thomas was exposed to asbestos-containing products or materials attributable to defendant. Defendant's objections to Mr. Verduzco's declaration are overruled. Defendant's objections to Mr. Ay's declaration are sustained. Even if the court considers Mr. Verduzco's declaration, or even if the court had continued this hearing to allow defendant to depose Mr. Verduzco and Mr. Verduzco affirmed the statements made in his declaration, it would be irrelevant because plaintiffs failed to present admissible evidence creating a triable issue whether the insulation at issue contained asbestos. Counsel for defendant submitted a proposed order to the Court to be considered together with defendant's evidentiary objections. Off the record both orders were signed. Judge: Teri L. Jackson, Reporter: Miles Lendway of AIKEN WELCH, CSR #13684 (Tel: 510-451-1580) (503/TLJ)
